mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) directly boost corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month changes (Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising unemployment and falling industrial production signals a demand-supply mismatch that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE change × INDPRO change) fails to predict SPX 20-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.04, or direction flips consistently across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained housing starts, supply chain bottlenecks persist, which supports corporate pricing power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns decline when HOUST falls below 500, or the coefficient on HOUST becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a regime-filtered regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding aggregate demand, which mechanically lifts earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth shows no predictive power for 5-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over any 5-year window) or produces negative forward returns in >55% of positive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting equity de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of instances where UNRATE jumps by more than 0.3pp within a calendar month.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in CPI and the federal funds rate indicates active inflation fighting that overshoots into equity valuation compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-product of month-over-month CPI and FEDFUNDS changes fails to explain more than 3% of the variance in 60-day SPX returns, or the sign of the interaction coefficient flips across out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and construction-related earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show no positive correlation with 5-day SPX returns during identified recovery windows, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling test.
